name: Why flights are more likely to be delayed at certain times of day
summary: Aviation experts weigh in on why the early bird doesn't just get the worm — it gets the on-time departure.

name: ‘A husband expects a yes’: how wife schools are shaping submissive Christian women
summary: A cottage industry of women are selling courses aligned with a conservative movement that claims feminism is the source of women’s discontent

name: TSA staffing crisis deepens as DHS funding nears cutoff
summary: More than 1,000 Transportation Security Administration officers have quit since the ongoing shutdown of the Department of Homeland Security began in February, with emergency funding that has kept workers paid about to run dry ahead of the World Cup and summer travel season.

name: Consciousness Generates Space-Time, Bold Theory Suggests—Meaning Reality Isn’t What It Seems
summary: Awareness itself may predate our universe.

name: Newsbrief | Nevada biz seeks thaw with gaming: Report
summary: Nevada's ailing cannabiz wants a better relationship with the casinos, the Las Vegas Sun reports. Since the state legalized REC in 2016, the casino industry, which answers to federal financial regulators, has distanced itself from the industry. The industry says that's part of the problem. The mandatory 1,500 feet between cannabis businesses and casinos keeps tourists away and invites the illegal market in.

name: Data shows a drop in Southern Nevada rents, but locals say they're not seeing savings
summary: New numbers from the UNLV Lied Center for Real Estate show apartment prices in Clark County are down 2.8% from last year and more than 6% from their 2022 peak. Data from Apartment List shows the average rent in Clark County now sits around $1,423, down about 3.5% from last year. Despite the cooling market, landlords are offering record-breaking incentives to fill empty units rather than slashing prices across the board. The Lied Center for Real Estate reports incentive rates in Clark County are now the highest on record since 2000.

name: Activist on Ruby Duncan's legacy: 'Don’t sit and be quiet and watch others be oppressed'
summary: Academics and activists in the Las Vegas Valley are remembering Ruby Duncan after her passing at the age of 93. She made it her mission to stand up for better services for needy families.

name: Chinese marketing firm embedded pro-Beijing narratives in Taiwan lifestyle pages, RSF finds
summary: An investigation by Reporters Without Borders has exposed how Wubianjie Group, a China-based digital marketing company, covertly injected pro-Beijing political messaging into Taiwanese lifestyle Facebook pages reaching tens of millions of users — a pattern corroborated by earlier Taiwanese government and academic research.

name: From cost reduction to cost prevention: powering iGaming’s AI transformation
summary: More than 80% of businesses in the space have embraced generative AI to support tasks like content creation and gathering customer insights, according to a new study by the University of Nevada, Las Vegas’ International Gaming Institute AI Research Hub in collaboration with audit, tax and advisory firm KPMG.
